name: Platelet EVs were safe but did not improve healthy-volunteer wound closure
summary: A first-in-human platelet-EV phase I trial found local injections well tolerated but no wound-closure advantage in healthy volunteer punch-biopsy wounds.
statement: In 11 healthy volunteers, LEAP-purified allogeneic platelet-derived extracellular vesicle injections were safe and well tolerated, but treated and placebo punch-biopsy wounds both healed in a mean 22.8 days and all closed by day 30.
population_or_model: Healthy adult volunteers with paired punch-biopsy wounds
time_horizon: Single treatment with 30-day safety and wound-closure follow-up
quantitative_note: No deaths, serious adverse events, or adverse events leading to withdrawal were reported; mean healing time was 22.8 +/- 8.7 days for both pEV and placebo wounds.
caveats: The trial was designed primarily for safety, not powered for efficacy.
Healthy acute wounds are not the delayed or disrupted wound-healing population targeted for future efficacy testing.
The result supports early human tolerability and manufacturing feasibility more than clinical benefit.
